messages.content: Congress passed the Federal Trade Commission Act, in part, to enforce antitrust laws.

While the Federal Trade Commission Act does give the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) the authority to enforce antitrust laws, that was not the sole purpose of the act. The act was passed in 1914 and created the FTC as an independent agency to regulate business practices and prevent unfair methods of competition in interstate commerce. The act also gave the FTC the power to investigate alleged violations of consumer protection laws, such as false advertising and fraud. So while enforcing antitrust laws is definitely one of the FTC's responsibilities, it is only part of a broader mandate to protect consumers and promote fair competition in the marketplace.

Nicole, who owns a nursery, contracts with Barbara, who runs a bed and breakfast, to periodically landscape and maintain the grounds of the bed and breakfast, but Barbara does not control Nicole's work, nor her designs and landscaping choices in performing the contract, and Nicole brings her own tools and crew to the bed and breakfast. Nicole is an
independent contractor.
Preferred stock owners often do not have a voting right in exchange for a higher dividend amount or preference in receiving dividends. true or false?

True. 

Preferred stock is a type of stock that typically offers a fixed dividend rate and priority in receiving dividend payments over common stockholders. However, preferred stockholders often do not have voting rights or have limited voting rights compared to common stockholders. This trade-off between dividends and voting rights is a key characteristic of preferred stock.
One advantage of the sole proprietorship is that the owner's losses are limited to the amount he or she has invested into the enterprise. true or false

False. 

In a sole proprietorship, the owner and the business are considered the same entity, which means that the owner is personally responsible for all of the business's liabilities and debts. As a result, if the business incurs losses or is sued, the owner's personal assets (such as their home, car, or savings) may be at risk. This is known as unlimited liability, which is a significant disadvantage of the sole proprietorship.
The Robinson-Patman Act prohibits first line, second line, and third line discrimination by the seller in all forms. true or false?

True. 

The Robinson-Patman Act is a federal law that prohibits price discrimination, which occurs when a seller charges different prices to different buyers for identical products or services. The law applies to all forms of discrimination, including first line, second line, and third line discrimination. First line discrimination refers to price differences offered to buyers of the same product at the same time. Second line discrimination refers to price differences offered to buyers at different times. And third line discrimination refers to price differences that result from differences in the quantity of products purchased. The Robinson-Patman Act prohibits all forms of price discrimination that harm competition and does not distinguish between different types of price discrimination.

Which of the following is a feature of the free enterprise system?
Group of answer choices

It is a socialist principle.

It is a public and involuntary system of production and distribution that involves significant governmental regulation.

It has worked so well and is self-regulating that it has not required any governmental intervention to protect workers, competition, and consumers.

It is a private and consensual system of production and distribution that is relatively free of governmental interference.
It is a private and consensual system of production and distribution that is relatively free of governmental interference.
What type of business organization calls for owners sharing profits equally?

A partnership is a type of business organization that calls for owners sharing profits equally. In a partnership, two or more individuals share ownership of a business and are responsible for its profits and losses. Partnerships can be formed with an agreement that specifies how profits and losses will be shared among the partners. In some partnerships, profits may be shared equally among the partners, while in others they may be shared based on a pre-determined percentage or formula.

Under Becca's specific duty of “loyalty” to Katherine, when Becca is Katherine's agent,
Group of answer choices

none of the above

Becca must perform all the lawful tasks Katherine assigns her.

Becca must notify Katherine of any activities involving the agency relationship.

Becca must act to serve Katherine's interests, and place Katherine's interests before her own, so that there is no conflict of interest.

Becca must act to serve Katherine's interests, and place Katherine's interests before her own, so that there is no conflict of interest. 

The duty of loyalty is a fundamental duty that an agent owes to their principal in an agency relationship. This duty requires the agent to act in the best interests of their principal, to avoid conflicts of interest, and to not use their position to further their own personal interests at the expense of the principal. In the context of this question, Becca (the agent) is required to place Katherine's (the principal) interests above her own, to avoid conflicts of interest, and to act in good faith at all times.
Vertical agreements, as a form of concerted action, are almost always struck down as per se violations of antitrust laws. true or false?

False. 

Vertical agreements, which are agreements between firms at different levels of the supply chain (such as between a manufacturer and a distributor), can be subject to antitrust scrutiny under certain circumstances. However, they are not always struck down as per se violations of antitrust laws. Instead, the legality of these agreements is typically analyzed under the rule of reason, which requires an assessment of the overall competitive effects of the agreement.

Under the rule of reason analysis, the court evaluates the procompetitive and anticompetitive effects of the agreement and conducts a balancing test to determine whether the net effect is a substantial lessening of competition in the relevant market. If the agreement is found to have anticompetitive effects that outweigh any procompetitive benefits, then it may be deemed illegal under antitrust laws.

Therefore, while vertical agreements are not always illegal, they may be subject to antitrust scrutiny and may be struck down if they have anticompetitive effects that outweigh their procompetitive benefits.

The business judgment rule protects officers and directors from
Group of answer choices

decisions made in their own self-interest or self-dealing.

decisions made in bad faith, as a result of fraud, through gross negligence, or as an abuse of discretion.

decisions not in the best interest of the corporation and shareholders.

decisions that were unprofitable or unsuccessful, as long as they were made in good faith, with due care, and within their authority.

Decisions that were unprofitable or unsuccessful, as long as they were made in good faith, with due care, and within their authority. 

The business judgment rule is a legal principle that governs the actions and decisions of officers and directors of corporations. Under this rule, officers and directors are protected from personal liability for their decisions as long as they are acting in good faith, with due care, and within the scope of their authority.

The rule provides a presumption that officers and directors acted in good faith and in the best interests of the corporation, and courts will not second-guess their decisions as long as they are made with care and in good faith. However, the rule does not protect decisions made in bad faith, as a result of fraud, through gross negligence, or as an abuse of discretion. Similarly, the rule does not protect decisions made in their own self-interest or self-dealing, or decisions not in the best interests of the corporation and shareholders.

Shareholders can be held liable for the corporation's actions, even above the amount of their investment in the corporation, under any of the following legal theories except:
Group of answer choices

alter ego theory

promotion of justice theory

piercing the corporate veil

unsuccessful investment theory

Promotion of justice theory. 

The legal concept of piercing the corporate veil allows a court to disregard the limited liability protection afforded to shareholders of a corporation and hold them personally liable for the debts and obligations of the corporation. This concept is typically invoked when a corporation is used as a shield to perpetrate fraud or when corporate formalities and requirements are not properly maintained.

However, shareholders cannot be held liable for the corporation’s actions above the amount of their investment in the corporation under the unsuccessful investment theory or promotion of justice theory. The unsuccessful investment theory suggests that a shareholder is not personally liable for corporate debts or obligations because their investment was unsuccessful. The promotion of justice theory is not a recognized legal theory, and it is not applicable to this question.

Alice, Bob, and Carol want to form a limited partnership to build two restaurants: Alice's Restaurant and Bob's Deli. They are the only partners in the business. What arrangement of partners is not acceptable?
Group of answer choices

Bob and Carol are general partners, and Alice is a limited partner.

Alice, Bob, and Carol are all general partners.

Alice and Bob are general partners, and Carol is a limited partners.

Alice is a general partner, and Bob and Carol are limited partners.

Alice, Bob, and Carol are all general partners. 

In a limited partnership, there must be at least one general partner and at least one limited partner. A general partner has unlimited personal liability for the partnership's debts and obligations, while a limited partner's liability is limited to the amount of their investment in the partnership.

In the options given, all of the arrangements include at least one general partner and at least one limited partner, except for the option where Alice, Bob, and Carol are all general partners. If all three partners are general partners, then there would be no limited partners in the business, and therefore, it would not be a limited partnership. This arrangement would not be acceptable under the rules governing limited partnerships.

Both the Department of Justice and the Federal Trade Commission may enforce antitrust laws by pursuing the following criminal remedies:
Group of answer choices

consent decree

divesture order

fine and/or imprisonment

injunction

Fine and/or imprisonment.

Both the Department of Justice and the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) may enforce antitrust laws by pursuing criminal remedies such as fines, imprisonment, or both. These remedies are typically used in cases of serious antitrust violations, such as price-fixing, bid-rigging, or market allocation schemes.

In addition to criminal remedies, the DOJ and FTC can also pursue civil remedies, such as injunctions, divestiture orders, or consent decrees. These remedies are typically used to address antitrust violations that are not as severe as criminal violations but still harm competition and consumers.
